displayName.text: KazuNori: The Original Hand Roll Bar

text.text: We've had both take away (because no dogs inside) and dine in. Take away is cut rolls only, no hand rolls, nicely presented in a bento-like fashioned box. Delicious. So delicious in fact, we returned for the dine in experience a week later. Clean cozy bar, good restroom (2 soaps, 1 for before and 1 for after to make your fishy hands smell better), a fine variety of music that isn't too loud and make you want to cut off your ears. Now for the hand rolls. We ordered the 6 piece Nozie special. It sends you, as the kids say. To a planet where fish is obviously honored for your taste. Okay that sounds off.  Anywho each piece hits a different part of your tastebuds so that they sing together in appreciation. It was so good that maybe only oishii! can describe it. Iykyk. I'd like to go back tomorrow please. Thank you, KazuNori.

text.text:
The new KazuNori location on Colorado Boulevard just opened, and we stopped by for lunch before heading to the Huntington Library. Overall, this was truly a 4.5 out of 5 experience.

We each ordered the 4 hand roll set. I added an extra yellowtail and substituted the crab for salmon, which was handled easily. The rice was seasoned perfectly, the fish was fresh, and the seaweed continues to be one of the best you can get for hand rolls.

The staff was friendly, and the hand rolls were presented right when you expected them, which kept the pacing of the meal spot on. Service was quick and efficient without feeling rushed. The ambiance is consistent and inviting, exactly what you expect from KazuNori.

My only minor wishes would be fresh wasabi as an option and seats that are a bit more form fitting, especially if you plan to sit through a full set. Neither is a deal breaker, but both would elevate the experience even more.

KazuNori remains one of the most reliable spots for high quality hand rolls, and this new Pasadena location is a great addition. I will definitely be back.

originalText.text:

text.text:
We tried the hand rolls with hot green tea, here and they were delicious. Everything tasted great and felt carefully prepared, and the presentation was nice too. The service was good and smooth, and the staff were friendly and attentive, which made the whole experience pleasant.

That said, we did have a bit of an issue afterward — we ended up burping for most of the afternoon after the meal, which was a little uncomfortable and unexpected.

originalText.text:

text.text:
If you know KazuNori, you know the drill, long lines, but totally worth the wait! This spot on Colorado Blvd is easily one of the most popular hand roll spots in LA, and for good reason.

The setup is all bar seating and it’s pretty limited, so definitely expect a bit of a hangout before you get a stool. Once you’re in, the service is great and the rolls come out fast and super fresh.

I went with the 6-roll set which included the lobster roll, so good! But honestly, the scallop roll was the sleeper hit for me; the flavor was spot on. I love that you can do the sets or just go à la carte if you have specific favorites.

Parking in this area can be a nightmare, but there’s a big garage nearby that makes it super easy.

Great food, fresh fish, and that signature crispy seaweed. I’ll definitely be back (once I have time to stand in line again)!

text.text: We savored the 6-Hand Roll and salmon sashimi, and it was truly delightful—especially the Bay Scallops Roll, which was exceptionally fresh and flavorful.
